channel.title: OpenAI on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in OpenAI on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: OpenAI’s GPT-5.1-Codex-Max is now in public preview for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub announced that OpenAI’s GPT-5.1-Codex-Max is rolling out in public preview for GitHub Copilot and will be available to Copilot Pro, Pro+, Business, and Enterprise customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OpenAI’s GPT-5.1, GPT-5.1-Codex and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini are now in public preview for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot has begun a public preview rollout of OpenAI’s latest 5.1-series models—GPT-5.1, GPT-5.1-Codex, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini—bringing the new general and code-specialized models (including a smaller, low‑cost/low‑latency Codex-Mini) to Copilot users. The announcement was posted on the GitHub Blog.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: vscode: A Unified Experience for all Coding Agents
item.description:
The article announces that coding agents have become central to VS Code in 2025, describing new agent mode, Copilot integration, and the GitHub Copilot CLI while noting the growing ecosystem of agents from providers like OpenAI and Anthropic.

  • Source: Visual Studio Code

item.title: security: SesameOp: Novel backdoor uses OpenAI Assistants API for command and control
item.description:
Microsoft DART researchers identified a novel backdoor, dubbed SesameOp, that abuses the OpenAI Assistants API as a covert command-and-control (C2) channel. A component of the backdoor uses the Assistants API as a storage/relay to fetch commands and execute tasks inside compromised environments, avoiding traditional C2 methods.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Selected Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini Copilot models are now deprecated
item.description:
On October 23, 2025, GitHub deprecated selected Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini models across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions), according to a GitHub Blog changelog post.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of select Copilot models from Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini
item.description:
GitHub announced it will deprecate select Copilot models provided by Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini as part of routine model retirement to prioritize newer, more capable models and maintain a fast, high‑quality Copilot experience. The post directs users to the changelog for the specific models and migration timeline and urges customers to update integrations to supported models.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OpenAI GPT-5-Codex is rolling out in public preview for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
OpenAI’s GPT-5-Codex, a model optimized for agentic coding, is rolling out in public preview for GitHub Copilot. The model will be available to Copilot Pro, Pro+, Business, and Enterprise users.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OpenAI GPT-5 and GPT-5 mini are now generally available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub announced that OpenAI’s GPT-5 and GPT-5 mini are now generally available in GitHub Copilot. The announcement explicitly states GPT-5 mini is available to all Copilot plans, including Copilot Free; the excerpt does not include full details about GPT-5 plan availability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OpenAI GPT-5 mini is now available in public preview in Visual Studio, JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse
item.description:
OpenAI’s GPT-5 mini is now publicly available in preview for GitHub Copilot users within Visual Studio, JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse.

  • Source: GitHub
